channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Jigawa boat accident death toll rises to six, 34 still missing
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The death toll from the Jigawa boat accident has risen to six, with 34 people still missing after a boat carrying 40 farmers capsized. Rescue operations ar
Read More: https://punchng.com/jigawa-boat-accident-death-toll-rises-to-six-34-still-missing/

item.title: Hearts, Rangers, Motherwell and Hibs find out Euro qualifying opponents
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Hearts and Rangers have found out their next qualifying opponents in the Champions League and Europa League.

item.title: PCN inducts 85 foreign-trained pharmacists
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Pharmacy Council of Nigeria inducted 85 foreign-trained pharmacists, charging them to uphold high standards as they begin practice in Nigeria.
Read More: https://punchng.com/pcn-inducts-85-foreign-trained-pharmacists/

item.description:
Edo State Government issues a 7MW mini-grid electricity licence to IZENERGY Limited, boosting access to electricity and driving industrial development in t

item.title: Court grants Miyetti Allah president Bodejo N2bn bail
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Miyetti Allah President Bello Bodejo has been granted N2bn bail by the Federal High Court in Abuja in his $2.63m money laundering trial.
Read More: https://punchng.com/court-grants-miyetti-allah-president-bodejo-n2bn-bail/

item.title: EFCC dismisses secret recruitment claims, warns public of viral fake news
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The EFCC has dismissed viral claims of secret recruitment, warning the public against fake news and scams. Verify all EFCC recruitment information official

item.title: Four police officers detained over alleged extortion of ICPC chairman in Abuja
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Police Command has detained four police officers over allegations that they extorted ₦53,000 from the Chairman of the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC), Dr. Musa Aliyu (SAN), during an unauthorised stop-and-search operation

item.title: EFCC Debunks Viral Recruitment Claim
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has dismissed as false a viral social media claim alleging that it is secretly recruiting new personnel. In a disclaimer posted on its official X page, the anti-graft agency described the report as “fake news” and cautioned members of t

item.title: Tech Trivia with Ada Oyom, Founder of She Code Africa
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: This is Technext’s weekly Women-in-tech trivia, a close-up series that spotlights the lives and personalities of female tech enthusiasts, professionals, and founders. A new edition drops every Monday.

item.title: Alleged Money Laundering: Court Admits Miyetti Allah President To N2bn Bail
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has admitted to bail the National President of Miyetti Allah Kautal Hore, Bello Bodejo, to the tune of N2 billion over alleged money laundering involving $2.63 million. Delivering the judgment after considering Bodejo’s bail application, Justice Inyang Ekwo held that

item.title: Nathaniel Bassey Hails Spain, Criticises Argentina’s Approach In World Cup Final
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Gospel singer Nathaniel Bassey has congratulated Spain on winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup, while criticising Argentina’s approach during the final, saying the South Americans spent more time engaging in theatrics than playing football. In a post on his official X page after Spain’s 1-0 e

item.title: Oil prices reverse lower, after report of 10-day ceasefire proposal
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices reversed lower after a report of a 10-day ceasefire proposal came after Iran’s foreign ministry said negotiations with the U.S. could be pursued.

item.title: SSG, Head of Service, Commissioners Absent As Governor Visits Empty Jigawa Secretariat
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A surprise inspection by Jigawa State Governor Umar Namadi has exposed widespread absenteeism at the State Secretariat, with the offices of top government officials locked and many senior civil servants nowhere to be found during official working hours. The post SSG, Head of Service, Commissioners A

item.title: World Cup 2026 in Numbers!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: How many goals, red cards and penalties? Take a look at the World Cup in 2026 in Numbers.

item.title: Fresh Benue attack: Gunmen shoot resident as troops launch manhunt
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Sector 1, Operation Whirl Stroke, OPWS, have launched a manhunt for suspected gunmen after responding to an attack on Azebiam community in Ukum Local Government Area of Benue State, where one resident was shot. The development was disclosed in a report by Zagazola Makama, a counter-insurge

item.title: Samsung Cuts 700+ US Jobs After Consumer Electronics Headquarters Move to Texas
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Samsung Electronics has reduced its US consumer electronics workforce after moving its headquarters from New Jersey to Texas, affecting hundreds of employees across both locations. The post Samsung Cuts 700+ US Jobs After Consumer Electronics Headquarters Move to Texas appeared first on Tech | Busin

item.title: Busha Business, Tether partner to expand stablecoin infrastructure for African businesses

item.description: Busha Business, the B2B infrastructure arm of Busha, has collaborated with Tether, the largest company in the digital asset industry read more Busha Business, Tether partner to expand stablecoin infrastructure for African businesses

item.title: Treasury bills, FGN bonds beat inflation as disinflation boosts real returns for investors
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria’s fixed-income market has entered a rare phase where government securities are once again generating returns above inflation, as easing consumer prices and elevated sovereign yields combine to restore positive real returns for investors. The post Treasury bills, FGN bonds beat inflation as d

item.title: Womenswear Brand Reformation, Backer Seek $239 Million in IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Reformation Inc., a sustainable womenswear brand, and shareholders including its private equity backer Permira are seeking to raise as much as $239 million in its US initial public offering.

item.title: EFCC debunks viral ‘Secret Recruitment’ rumour, urges Nigerians to ignore false claim
item.dc:creator: Vanguard

The commission issued the clarification on Monday through its verified X account, warning Nigerians not to be misled b

item.title: National Grid: NERC issues guidelines for electricity transmission audit

item.description: The Nigerian Electricity Regulatory Commission, NERC has issued guidelines for the Technical Audit of the Electricity Transmission System and Network Data Validation, 2026. NERC disclosed this in a notice posted on its official X account on Monday. The regulator said the move is pursuant to Sections

item.title: Troops Arrest Sudanese Terror Suspect, Ghanaian Illegal Immigrant In Borno
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have arrested a Sudanese national suspected to be a foreign terrorist fighter and collaborator, alongside an alleged illegal immigrant from Ghana, during a security operation in Askira/Uba Local Government Area of Borno State. item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles striker Victor Boniface has admitted that the last year has been one of the most difficult periods of his career after finally ending his Bayer 04 Leverkusen goalscoring drought, Soccernet.ng reports. The 25-year-old scored twice in Bayer Leverkusen's emphatic 7-0 pre-season victory ove

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Canadian rapper Drake has lost a $1.5 million bet, worth more than N2 billion, after Argentina suffered a 1-0 defeat to Spain in the final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post Drake suffers over N2bn betting loss after Argentina’s defeat appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: 'Last week, I let a passenger go, and the money never came. I lost that fuel money. item.title: Long-Term Tactical Approach To Take At No Deposit Gaming Platforms
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The highly competitive nature of the contemporary digital gaming market has fundamentally altered how players interact with promotional incentives. No deposit bonuses, once viewed as rare anomalies designed purely to generate short-term interest, have been repositioned as foundational strategic tool

item.title: NASS Roundup: State police, N34tn waivers and other stories from the House of Reps
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The lawmakers began fresh consideration of a state police bill, demanded explanations over alleged unremitted public revenues and import duty waivers, while discussions at the House Open Week focused on legislative oversight, women’s representation, youth participation and improving Nigeria’s invest

item.title: ‘Una Go Qualify?’ – Nigerian Fans Troll Super Eagles Over ‘See You In 2030’ 2026 World Cup Victory Congratulatory Message To Spain
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Nigerian football fans have criticised the Super Eagles after the team’s official X account congratulated Spain on winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup while declaring, “See you in 2030.” The post, shared shortly after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time to lift the World Cup on Sunday, quickly

item.title: 30 feared dead as canoe capsized in Jigawa
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: No fewer than 30 persons are feared to have lost their lives after a canoe conveying farmers and other residents capsized on Sunday in Malamawar Yandutse community, The post 30 feared dead as canoe capsized in Jigawa appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Why is Iran bombing Gulf states while avoiding US naval assets and Israel?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Analysts say Iran is using Gulf states to pressure Washington while avoiding direct confrontation with US forces.

item.title: Photos: Police fire tear gas at India’s Cockroach movement supporters
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The youth-led Cockroach Janta Party protests in New Delhi, demanding India's education minister resign over exam leaks.

item.title: Onoh commends Olukoyede for EFCC’s largest ever recovery in Nigeria history
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Dennis Agbo Former Southeast Spokesman to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and Chairman of the Forum of Former Members of the Enugu State House of Assembly, Denge Josef Onoh, has commended the Executive Chairman of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), Mr. Ola Olukoyede, and the officer

item.title: Gas Plants’ Condensate Output Hit 2.228m Barrels In June – NMDPRA
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria’s gas processing plants produced 2.228 million barrels of condensates in June 2026, the highest monthly output so far this year, according to the latest statistics released by the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority (NMDPRA). The figure represents a steady

item.title: 'Rogers move is crazy money for a player Chelsea don't need!'
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: In his latest column, Sky Sports' Paul Merson gives his reaction to Morgan Rogers' stunning £117m move to Chelsea.

item.title: How policemen forced ICPC Chairman to part with money at Abuja checkpoint
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Police Command has arrested four police officers accused of forcing the Chairman of the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC), Dr. Musa Aliyu (SAN), to part with ₦53,000 at an illegal checkpoint in Abuja. The officers, who alle

item.title: ‘I go with a smile’ says outgoing UK PM Keir Starmer in final speech
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: ‘I go with a smile’ says outgoing UK PM Keir Starmer in final speech

item.title: Enugu to distribute farm inputs to 63,000 farmers, recruit 4,698 teachers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Enugu State Government will on Tuesday distribute farm inputs to 63,000 smallholder farmers across the state to boost agricultural production this farming season. The post Enugu to distribute farm inputs to 63,000 farmers, recruit 4,698 teachers appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Nigeria’s central bank is rewriting the rules for fintech growth
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Over the past decade, the playbook for Nigerian fintechs has been remarkably consistent: build payment products, acquire merchants, scale transaction volumes, obtain microfinance bank licences, expand into lending, and eventually launch savings products.

item.title: Why ending insecurity is difficult in Nigeria — Bwala
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Policy Communications, Daniel Bwala, has attributed Nigeria's prolonged security challenges to a combination of foreign interests and cross-border terrorist activities, saying these factors have made it difficult to end insecurity completely. The post
